name: ans
description: Approximate Number System — magnitude comparison via graph structure

nodes.description: Receives activation proportional to quantity A
Receives activation proportional to quantity B
Accumulates magnitude of A. Activation level = perceived magnitude.
Accumulates magnitude of B. Activation level = perceived magnitude.
Computes difference between accumulator activations. Inhibitory competition.
Active when A > B (by sufficient margin)
Active when B > A (by sufficient margin)
Active when A ≈ B (within Weber fraction)

edges.description: Inhibitory — subtracts B from A at comparator
Lateral connection for mutual inhibition / comparison

properties.description: Weber fraction determines discrimination threshold. Adult humans ≈ 0.15. Ratio-dependent: 10 vs 5 (ratio 2) is easy, 10 vs 9 (ratio 1.11) is hard. The weber fraction is NOT implemented as code — it emerges from the inhibitory competition between accumulator nodes and the mutual inhibition between output nodes. The weight magnitudes on the comparator edges determine the effective weber fraction.
